notice: Maintenance & failover truth: every notice declares whether it is a maintenance, drain, failover, or tenant-migration window, the exact window time / timezone / offset, the affected deployment scope and write classes, which queued publish-later or local-draft work survives (kept separate from successful hosted mutations), what stays local-safe, and any changed tenant / region / endpoint boundary. A notice reads as current only while it is active and its last refresh is current — otherwise it downgrades and names why, and never collapses into a generic degraded banner. Shell, activity history, CLI / headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports read this record verbatim.
title: Control-plane failover to the standby plane
summary: The control plane failed over; key ownership needs a recheck before policy writes.

affected_scope.scope_summary: Managed-cloud auth/policy control plane failed over to standby.

axes.summary: Key ownership unknown after failover; recheck before policy writes.

boundary_change.summary: Key ownership requires a recheck after the control-plane failover.

blocked_writes.note: Remote sessions must reconnect to the standby plane.
AI gateway prompts retry once the standby plane is reachable.

retained_local_safe_capabilities: Editing, saving, and local Git continue offline.
local_continuity.continuity_summary: Local-only work continues; managed control-plane actions wait for reconnect.
